chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يَجِبُ مِنَ الِاقْتِدَاءِ بِالْأَئِمَّةِ ( عليهم السلام ) فِي التَّعَرُّضِ لِلرِّزْقِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 4 – What is Obligated from the emulating the Imams<sup>asws</sup> regarding the vulnerability of the livelihood

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/17)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/17)
text: أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) أَعْتَقَ أَلْفَ مَمْلُوكٍ مِنْ كَدِّ يَدِهِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, form Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Sayf Bin Ameyra, and Salma Sahib Al Sabiry, from Abu Asama Zayd Al Shahaam,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> that Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> freed a thousand owned slaves from the toil of his<sup>asws</sup> own hands’.
en.sarwar: 4. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Sayf ibn ‘Amirah and Salmah Sahib al-Sabiriy from abu ‘Usamah Zayd al-Shahham who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that ’Amir al-Mu’minin, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, had set free one thousand slaves by means of what he had earned with his own hands.”
